CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str VARCHAR(100));
SELECT rtrim(str, 'a') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT rtrim(str, 'b') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 5;
SELECT id, rtrim(str, 'a') AS trimmed FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3);
WITH trimmed_strings AS (SELECT id, rtrim(str, 'a') AS trimmed FROM test_strings) SELECT id, trimmed FROM trimmed_strings WHERE trimmed <> '';
SELECT rtrim((SELECT str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3), 'f') AS result;
SELECT t1.id, rtrim(t2.str, 'b') AS trimmed FROM test_strings AS t1 JOIN test_strings AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.id <= 3;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE rtrim(str, 'a') = '';
SELECT rtrim(CONCAT('abcdef', str), '1') FROM (SELECT str FROM test_strings WHERE id = 6) AS subquery;
SELECT rtrim(s1.str, 'z') FROM (SELECT str FROM test_strings) AS s1 WHERE s1.str IS NOT NULL;
SELECT rtrim(COALESCE(NULLIF(str, ''), 'default'), 'default') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
